title: Bulk report - Week 40
summary:
Capesize
It was a surprising start to the week, with rates moving in some key areas despite a week of Asian holidays, although some moves were attributed to draft issues and rising bunker values. On the West Australia/China run rates for Dampier cargoes dropped under $8.00 the week before, jumping to $8.50 by Monday and near the mid $9.00s mid-week, although drafts played a part. The bubble was brief, with rates finishing the week near the mid $8.00s.

children.title: Capesize
children.summary: It was a surprising start to the week, with rates moving in some key areas despite a week of Asian holidays, although some moves were attributed to draft issues and rising bunker values. On the West Australia/China run rates for Dampier cargoes dropped under $8.00 the week before, jumping to $8.50 by Monday and near the mid $9.00s mid-week, although drafts played a part.

children.title: Panamax
children.summary: Last week defied expectation, with a long holiday in China many expected a slow week with little excitement. Monday was slow with others also away, but as the week progressed, both activity and rates increased.

children.title: Supramax
children.summary: Despite widespread holidays mainly affecting the Asian market, generally rates remained firm. Period activity was steady with a 61,400dwt fixing in the mid $15,000s for four to six months trading delivery Indian Ocean.

children.title: Handysize
children.summary: Continuing the positive trend from September, the BHSI climbed throughout the week despite the holidays in Asia and Germany. The US Gulf market remained firm, with trans-Atlantic trips from the Gulf fixed largely on the bigger-sized handy vessels, and the gap between the 38,000dwt and 28,000dwt index vessels widened.
